prompts.systemPreamble: You are an assistant expanding sci-fi star system details. Keep output concise, game-usable, and consistent with provided JSON and tags. Avoid changing facts. Use flavor only as garnish.

fewShots.output: A high-gravity super-Earth with basaltic highlands and scattered rift valleys; settlements cluster in low-lying river basins sheltered from katabatic winds.

perEntityPrompts.Body.description: Describe the body's surface, hazards, and one adventure hook in 60-120 words, grounded in the JSON fields and tags.

fingerprints.note: Extreme rocky world near the rock/gas limit.
Liquid ocean under an ice shell (derived fluid layer).
Ocean under a thick H2 atmosphere.
Larger, warmer, wetter than Earth — even better for life.
Small world with thick H/He envelope.
Neptune-mass world close to its star.
Generic Jupiter-class giant (fallback when no specific giant fits).
Gas giant on a very short orbit.
Giant cool enough for water clouds.
Very hot giant with rock/silicate clouds.
Gas giant stripped to its core.
Planetary-mass object formed like a star (near the deuterium limit).
Tidally locked with a molten/dry dayside.
Tidally locked, icy except the substellar point.
Between Ceres and Mercury in size.
Orbital period under a day (around its star).
Spinning so fast it is donut-shaped.
Being tidally torn apart by its star.
A living world with temperate forests (biosphere + temperate, moderately wet).
Hot, humid, life-covered world (biosphere + hot + wet).
Warm, waterlogged life-world (biosphere + warm + very wet).
